title: Platanthera cooperi
intro: Platanthera cooperi, also known as Cooper's orchid or Platte's orchid, is a species of orchid native to North America, characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender stem. The health benefits of Platanthera cooperi include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenylethanoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Platanthera cooperi is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Platanthera cooperi is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Platanthera. The genus Platanthera is part of the subtribe Orchideae and the tribe Orchideae. This orchid species belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The subfamily Orchidoideae also encompasses Platanthera cooperi. It is native to North America.
